query:
-- SCIENCE-1: Biology (15 questions)
INSERT INTO bridge_academy_practice_questions (topicId, question, questionType, options, correctAnswer, explanation, difficulty, variationGroup) VALUES
(6, 'What is the basic unit of life?', 'multiple_choice', '[\"Atom\", \"Cell\", \"Molecule\", \"Organ\"]', 'Cell', 'The cell is the smallest unit of life', 'easy', 'topic_6_group_0'),
(6, 'What is photosynthesis?', 'multiple_choice', '[\"Breaking down glucose\", \"Process where plants make food using sunlight\", \"Respiration\", \"Digestion\"]', 'Process where plants make food using sunlight', 'Photosynthesis converts light energy into chemical energy', 'medium', 'topic_6_group_0'),
(6, 'What are the three main parts of a cell?', 'multiple_choice', '[\"Nucleus, cytoplasm, cell membrane\", \"Mitochondria, ribosome, DNA\", \"Chloroplast, cell wall, vacuole\", \"Nucleus, ribosome, mitochondria\"]', 'Nucleus, cytoplasm, cell membrane', 'All cells have these three main components', 'easy', 'topic_6_group_1'),
(6, 'What is DNA?', 'multiple_choice', '[\"A protein\", \"Deoxyribonucleic acid that carries genetic information\", \"An enzyme\", \"A carbohydrate\"]', 'Deoxyribonucleic acid that carries genetic information', 'DNA is the molecule that stores genetic instructions', 'medium', 'topic_6_group_1'),
(6, 'What is the function of mitochondria?', 'multiple_choice', '[\"Photosynthesis\", \"Protein synthesis\", \"Energy production\", \"Storage\"]', 'Energy production', 'Mitochondria is the powerhouse of the cell', 'medium', 'topic_6_group_2'),
(6, 'What is an enzyme?', 'multiple_choice', '[\"A hormone\", \"A protein that speeds up chemical reactions\", \"A carbohydrate\", \"A lipid\"]', 'A protein that speeds up chemical reactions', 'Enzymes are biological catalysts', 'medium', 'topic_6_group_2'),
(6, 'What is homeostasis?', 'multiple_choice', '[\"Change\", \"Maintenance of stable internal conditions\", \"Growth\", \"Reproduction\"]', 'Maintenance of stable internal conditions', 'Homeostasis is the body\'s ability to maintain equilibrium', 'medium', 'topic_6_group_3'),
(6, 'What is the function of ribosomes?', 'multiple_choice', '[\"Energy production\", \"Protein synthesis\", \"Photosynthesis\", \"Storage\"]', 'Protein synthesis', 'Ribosomes are the site of protein production', 'medium', 'topic_6_group_3'),
(6, 'What is the difference between prokaryotes and eukaryotes?', 'multiple_choice', '[\"Size difference\", \"Prokaryotes lack a nucleus; eukaryotes have one\", \"Prokaryotes are larger\", \"No difference\"]', 'Prokaryotes lack a nucleus; eukaryotes have one', 'This is the key structural difference between these cell types', 'hard', 'topic_6_group_4'),
(6, 'What is cellular respiration?', 'multiple_choice', '[\"Photosynthesis\", \"Process of breaking down glucose to release energy\", \"Digestion\", \"Breathing\"]', 'Process of breaking down glucose to release energy', 'Cellular respiration produces ATP for the cell', 'hard', 'topic_6_group_4'),
(6, 'What is a chromosome?', 'multiple_choice', '[\"A protein\", \"A structure containing DNA\", \"An enzyme\", \"A carbohydrate\"]', 'A structure containing DNA', 'Chromosomes carry genetic information', 'medium', 'topic_6_group_5'),
(6, 'What is the role of the cell membrane?', 'multiple_choice', '[\"Energy production\", \"Control of what enters and exits the cell\", \"Protein synthesis\", \"Photosynthesis\"]', 'Control of what enters and exits the cell', 'The cell membrane is selectively permeable', 'medium', 'topic_6_group_5'),
(6, 'What is mutation?', 'multiple_choice', '[\"Evolution\", \"A change in DNA sequence\", \"Reproduction\", \"Adaptation\"]', 'A change in DNA sequence', 'Mutations are changes in genetic material', 'hard', 'topic_6_group_6'),
(6, 'What is the function of the nucleus?', 'multiple_choice', '[\"Energy production\", \"Control of cell activities and storage of genetic information\", \"Protein synthesis\", \"Photosynthesis\"]', 'Control of cell activities and storage of genetic information', 'The nucleus is the control center of the cell', 'easy', 'topic_6_group_6'),
(6, 'What is an allele?', 'multiple_choice', '[\"A gene\", \"A variant form of a gene\", \"A chromosome\", \"A protein\"]', 'A variant form of a gene', 'Alleles are different versions of the same gene', 'hard', 'topic_6_group_7');

-- SCIENCE-2: Chemistry & Physics (15 questions)
INSERT INTO bridge_academy_practice_questions (topicId, question, questionType, options, correctAnswer, explanation, difficulty, variationGroup) VALUES
(7, 'What is an atom?', 'multiple_choice', '[\"A molecule\", \"The smallest unit of an element\", \"A cell\", \"A compound\"]', 'The smallest unit of an element', 'Atoms are the building blocks of matter', 'easy', 'topic_7_group_0'),
(7, 'What is the periodic table?', 'multiple_choice', '[\"A table of elements\", \"Organized list of all known elements\", \"A chemistry textbook\", \"A list of compounds\"]', 'Organized list of all known elements', 'The periodic table organizes elements by properties', 'medium', 'topic_7_group_0'),
(7, 'What is a chemical bond?', 'multiple_choice', '[\"A physical connection\", \"A force holding atoms together\", \"An element\", \"A reaction\"]', 'A force holding atoms together', 'Chemical bonds link atoms in molecules', 'medium', 'topic_7_group_1'),
(7, 'What is the difference between an element and a compound?', 'multiple_choice', '[\"Size difference\", \"Elements have one type of atom; compounds have multiple\", \"Compounds are larger\", \"No difference\"]', 'Elements have one type of atom; compounds have multiple', 'This is the fundamental difference', 'hard', 'topic_7_group_1'),
(7, 'What is pH?', 'multiple_choice', '[\"Temperature\", \"Measure of acidity or alkalinity\", \"Pressure\", \"Density\"]', 'Measure of acidity or alkalinity', 'pH scale ranges from 0-14', 'medium', 'topic_7_group_2'),
(7, 'What is an acid?', 'multiple_choice', '[\"A base\", \"A substance that donates protons\", \"A salt\", \"A gas\"]', 'A substance that donates protons', 'Acids have a pH less than 7', 'medium', 'topic_7_group_2'),
(7, 'What is Newton\'s first law of motion?', 'multiple_choice', '[\"F=ma\", \"Objects in motion stay in motion unless acted upon by force\", \"Action-reaction\", \"Gravity\"]', 'Objects in motion stay in motion unless acted upon by force', 'This is the law of inertia', 'medium', 'topic_7_group_3'),
(7, 'What is force?', 'multiple_choice', '[\"Energy\", \"A push or pull on an object\", \"Motion\", \"Speed\"]', 'A push or pull on an object', 'Force is measured in Newtons', 'easy', 'topic_7_group_3'),
(7, 'What is velocity?', 'multiple_choice', '[\"Speed\", \"Speed in a specific direction\", \"Acceleration\", \"Distance\"]', 'Speed in a specific direction', 'Velocity is a vector quantity', 'medium', 'topic_7_group_4'),
(7, 'What is acceleration?', 'multiple_choice', '[\"Speed\", \"Change in velocity over time\", \"Distance\", \"Force\"]', 'Change in velocity over time', 'Acceleration can be positive or negative', 'medium', 'topic_7_group_4'),
(7, 'What is energy?', 'multiple_choice', '[\"Force\", \"Capacity to do work\", \"Motion\", \"Heat\"]', 'Capacity to do work', 'Energy exists in many forms', 'easy', 'topic_7_group_5'),
(7, 'What is the law of conservation of energy?', 'multiple_choice', '[\"Energy can be created\", \"Energy cannot be created or destroyed, only transformed\", \"Energy disappears\", \"Energy increases\"]', 'Energy cannot be created or destroyed, only transformed', 'This is a fundamental law of physics', 'hard', 'topic_7_group_5'),
(7, 'What is wavelength?', 'multiple_choice', '[\"Frequency\", \"Distance between wave crests\", \"Wave speed\", \"Amplitude\"]', 'Distance between wave crests', 'Wavelength is inversely related to frequency', 'hard', 'topic_7_group_6'),
(7, 'What is the speed of light?', 'multiple_choice', '[\"300,000 m/s\", \"3 x 10^8 m/s\", \"186,000 miles/second\", \"All of the above\"]', 'All of the above', 'These are all equivalent expressions', 'medium', 'topic_7_group_6'),
(7, 'What is an isotope?', 'multiple_choice', '[\"An element\", \"Atoms of the same element with different numbers of neutrons\", \"A compound\", \"A molecule\"]', 'Atoms of the same element with different numbers of neutrons', 'Isotopes have different mass numbers', 'hard', 'topic_7_group_7');

-- SCIENCE-2: Chemistry & Physics (15 questions)
INSERT INTO bridge_academy_practice_questions (topicId, question, questionType, options, correctAnswer, explanation, difficulty, variationGroup) VALUES
(7, 'What is an atom?', 'multiple_choice', '[\"A molecule\", \"The smallest unit of an element\", \"A cell\", \"A compound\"]', 'The smallest unit of an element', 'Atoms are the building blocks of matter', 'easy', 'topic_7_group_0'),
(7, 'What is the periodic table?', 'multiple_choice', '[\"A table of elements\", \"Organized list of all known elements\", \"A chemistry textbook\", \"A list of compounds\"]', 'Organized list of all known elements', 'The periodic table organizes elements by properties', 'medium', 'topic_7_group_0'),
(7, 'What is a chemical bond?', 'multiple_choice', '[\"A physical connection\", \"A force holding atoms together\", \"An element\", \"A reaction\"]', 'A force holding atoms together', 'Chemical bonds link atoms in molecules', 'medium', 'topic_7_group_1'),
(7, 'What is the difference between an element and a compound?', 'multiple_choice', '[\"Size difference\", \"Elements have one type of atom; compounds have multiple\", \"Compounds are larger\", \"No difference\"]', 'Elements have one type of atom; compounds have multiple', 'This is the fundamental difference', 'hard', 'topic_7_group_1'),
(7, 'What is pH?', 'multiple_choice', '[\"Temperature\", \"Measure of acidity or alkalinity\", \"Pressure\", \"Density\"]', 'Measure of acidity or alkalinity', 'pH scale ranges from 0-14', 'medium', 'topic_7_group_2'),
(7, 'What is an acid?', 'multiple_choice', '[\"A base\", \"A substance that donates protons\", \"A salt\", \"A gas\"]', 'A substance that donates protons', 'Acids have a pH less than 7', 'medium', 'topic_7_group_2'),
(7, 'What is Newton\'s first law of motion?', 'multiple_choice', '[\"F=ma\", \"Objects in motion stay in motion unless acted upon by force\", \"Action-reaction\", \"Gravity\"]', 'Objects in motion stay in motion unless acted upon by force', 'This is the law of inertia', 'medium', 'topic_7_group_3'),
(7, 'What is force?', 'multiple_choice', '[\"Energy\", \"A push or pull on an object\", \"Motion\", \"Speed\"]', 'A push or pull on an object', 'Force is measured in Newtons', 'easy', 'topic_7_group_3'),
(7, 'What is velocity?', 'multiple_choice', '[\"Speed\", \"Speed in a specific direction\", \"Acceleration\", \"Distance\"]', 'Speed in a specific direction', 'Velocity is a vector quantity', 'medium', 'topic_7_group_4'),
(7, 'What is acceleration?', 'multiple_choice', '[\"Speed\", \"Change in velocity over time\", \"Distance\", \"Force\"]', 'Change in velocity over time', 'Acceleration can be positive or negative', 'medium', 'topic_7_group_4'),
(7, 'What is energy?', 'multiple_choice', '[\"Force\", \"Capacity to do work\", \"Motion\", \"Heat\"]', 'Capacity to do work', 'Energy exists in many forms', 'easy', 'topic_7_group_5'),
(7, 'What is the law of conservation of energy?', 'multiple_choice', '[\"Energy can be created\", \"Energy cannot be created or destroyed, only transformed\", \"Energy disappears\", \"Energy increases\"]', 'Energy cannot be created or destroyed, only transformed', 'This is a fundamental law of physics', 'hard', 'topic_7_group_5'),
